steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content: The word “conversely” signals that the blank must express the opposite of “historically significant.”

steps.title: Match the opposing concept
steps.content:
The blank needs a word meaning the opposite of "historically significant," since "conversely" signals a contrast. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Momentous: of great importance — this reinforces "historically significant" rather than contrasting it; "conversely" demands the opposite. ✘
  • Choice B — Inconsequential: lacking significance or importance — the direct opposite of "historically significant," fitting the contrast signaled by "conversely." ✔
  • Choice C — Benevolent: kind and generous — this describes a ruler's character, not the historical weight of a reign; it doesn't contrast with "significant." ✘
  • Choice D — Genuine: authentic or real — describes authenticity, not historical importance; no contrast with "significant" is established. ✘
That matches choice B — B is correct.
